name: Current Employee Names, Salaries, and Position Titles

columns.name: Full or Part-Time
columns.description: Whether the employee was employed full- (F) or part-time (P).

columns.name: Job Titles
columns.description: Title of employee at the time when the data were updated.

columns.name: Typical Hours
columns.description:
Describes the typical amount of work for hourly employees. This data does not apply to salary employees.

40 - Employee paid on an hourly basis; works an 8 hour day; can be either full-time permanent (FT/P) or full-time temporary (FT-T) which is a seasonal employee; 35 - Employee paid on an hourly basis; works a 7 hour day; can be either full-time permanent (FT/P) or full-time temporary (FT-T) which is a seasonal employee; 20 - Employee paid on a part-time, hourly basis; typically works a 4 hour day, 5 days a week; 10 - Employee paid on a part-time, hourly basis; works 10 hours or less in a week.

columns.name: Hourly Rate
columns.description: The hourly salary rates for individuals whose pay frequency is "hourly". Hourly employees have varying hours worked throughout the week, which is described in the "Frequency Description" column.

columns.name: Annual Salary
columns.description: Annual salary rates. Only applies for employees whose pay frequency is "Salary". Hourly employees rates are only shown in "Hourly Rates" column.

columns.name: Name
columns.description: Name of employee

columns.name: Department
columns.description: Department where employee worked.

columns.name: Salary or Hourly
columns.description: Defines whether an employee is paid on an hourly basis or salary basis. Hourly employees are further defined by the number of hours they work in a week. See the "Frequency Description" column.

description: This dataset is a listing of all active City of Chicago employees, complete with full names, departments, positions, employment status (part-time or full-time), frequency of hourly employee –where applicable—and annual salaries or hourly rate. Please note that "active" has a specific meaning for Human Resources purposes and will sometimes exclude employees on certain types of temporary leave. For hourly employees, the City is providing the hourly rate and frequency of hourly employees (40, 35, 2

This column replaces columns PIN1 through PIN10, which will be removed soon, no earlier than 6/1/2024.

description: Note, 10/15/2025: We have added a PERMIT_CONDITION column. This dataset includes information about building permits issued by the City of Chicago from 2006 to the present, excluding permits that have been voided or revoked after issuance. Most types of permits are issued subject to payment of the applicable permit fee. Work under a permit may not begin until the applicable permit fee is paid. For more information about building permits, see http://www.chicago.gov/permit.

name: Crimes - 2001 to Present

columns.name: Beat
columns.description:
Indicates the beat where the incident occurred.  A beat is the smallest police geographic area – each beat has a dedicated police beat car.  Three to five beats make up a police sector, and three sectors make up a police district.  The Chicago Police Department has 22 police districts.  

See the beats at https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/aerh-rz74.

columns.name: IUCR
columns.description: The Illinois Uniform Crime Reporting code. This is directly linked to the Primary Type and Description. See the list of IUCR codes at https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/c7ck-438e.

columns.name: Updated On
columns.description: Date and time the record was last updated.

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: The latitude of the location where the incident occurred. This location is shifted from the actual location for partial redaction but falls on the same block.

columns.name: Block
columns.description: The partially redacted address where the incident occurred, placing it on the same block as the actual address.

columns.name: X Coordinate
columns.description: The x coordinate of the location where the incident occurred in State Plane Illinois East NAD 1983 projection.  This location is shifted from the actual location for partial redaction but falls on the same block.

columns.name: District
columns.description: Indicates the police district where the incident occurred.  See the districts at https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/fthy-xz3r.

columns.name: Domestic
columns.description: Indicates whether the incident was domestic-related as defined by the Illinois Domestic Violence Act.

columns.name: Date
columns.description: Date when the incident occurred. this is sometimes a best estimate.

columns.name: Description
columns.description: The secondary description of the IUCR code, a subcategory of the primary description.

columns.name: Case Number
columns.description: The Chicago Police Department RD Number (Records Division Number), which is unique to the incident.

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: The longitude of the location where the incident occurred. This location is shifted from the actual location for partial redaction but falls on the same block.

columns.name: Arrest
columns.description: Indicates whether an arrest was made.

columns.name: ID
columns.description: Unique identifier for the record.

columns.name: Year
columns.description: Year the incident occurred.

columns.name: Primary Type
columns.description: The primary description of the IUCR code.

columns.name: Y Coordinate
columns.description: The y coordinate of the location where the incident occurred in State Plane Illinois East NAD 1983 projection. This location is shifted from the actual location for partial redaction but falls on the same block.

columns.name: Ward
columns.description: The ward (City Council district) where the incident occurred.  See the wards at https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/sp34-6z76.

columns.name: Location Description
columns.description: Description of the location where the incident occurred.

columns.name: Community Area
columns.description:
Indicates the community area where the incident occurred.  Chicago has 77 community areas. 

See the community areas at https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/cauq-8yn6.

columns.name: FBI Code
columns.description: Indicates the crime classification as outlined in the FBI's National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS).See the Chicago Police Department listing of these classifications at https://gis.chicagopolice.org/pages/crime_details.

columns.name: Wards 2023-
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'Wards 2023-' (8hcu-yrd4) the point in column 'location' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: The location where the incident occurred in a format that allows for creation of maps and other geographic operations on this data portal. This location is shifted from the actual location for partial redaction but falls on the same block.

description: This dataset reflects reported incidents of crime (with the exception of murders where data exists for each victim) that occurred in the City of Chicago from 2001 to present, minus the most recent seven days. Data is extracted from the Chicago Police Department's CLEAR (Citizen Law Enforcement Analysis and Reporting) system. In order to protect the privacy of crime victims, addresses are shown at the block level only and specific locations are not identified. Should you have questions about this

name: Traffic Crashes - Crashes

columns.name: DOORING_I
columns.description: Whether crash involved a motor vehicle occupant opening a door into the travel path of a bicyclist, causing a crash

columns.name: ROAD_DEFECT
columns.description: Road defects, as determined by reporting officer

columns.name: STATEMENTS_TAKEN_I
columns.description: Whether statements were taken from unit(s) involved in crash

columns.name: PHOTOS_TAKEN_I
columns.description: Whether the Chicago Police Department took photos at the location of the crash

columns.name: BEAT_OF_OCCURRENCE
columns.description: Chicago Police Department Beat ID. Boundaries available at https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/aerh-rz74

columns.name: STREET_NAME
columns.description: Street address name of crash location, as determined by reporting officer

columns.name: WORK_ZONE_TYPE
columns.description: The type of work zone, if any

columns.name: STREET_DIRECTION
columns.description: Street address direction (N,E,S,W) of crash location, as determined by reporting officer

columns.name: STREET_NO
columns.description: Street address number of crash location, as determined by reporting officer

columns.name: SEC_CONTRIBUTORY_CAUSE
columns.description: The factor which was second most significant in causing the crash, as determined by officer judgment

columns.name: PRIM_CONTRIBUTORY_CAUSE
columns.description: The factor which was most significant in causing the crash, as determined by officer judgment

columns.name: INJURIES_NO_INDICATION
columns.description: Total persons sustaining no injuries in the crash as determined by the reporting officer

columns.name: INJURIES_INCAPACITATING
columns.description: Total persons sustaining incapacitating/serious injuries in the crash as determined by the reporting officer. Any injury other than fatal injury, which prevents the injured person from walking, driving, or normally continuing the activities they were capable of performing before the injury occurred. Includes severe lacerations, broken limbs, skull or chest injuries, and abdominal injuries.

columns.name: INJURIES_NON_INCAPACITATING
columns.description: Total persons sustaining non-incapacitating injuries in the crash as determined by the reporting officer. Any injury, other than fatal or incapacitating injury, which is evident to observers at the scene of the crash. Includes lump on head, abrasions, bruises, and minor lacerations.

columns.name: INJURIES_TOTAL
columns.description: Total persons sustaining fatal, incapacitating, non-incapacitating, and possible injuries as determined by the reporting officer

columns.name: MOST_SEVERE_INJURY
columns.description: Most severe injury sustained by any person involved in the crash

columns.name: WORK_ZONE_I
columns.description: Whether the crash occurred in an active work zone

columns.name: CRASH_RECORD_ID
columns.description: This number can be used to link to the same crash in the Vehicles and People datasets. This number also serves as a unique ID in this dataset.

columns.name: INTERSECTION_RELATED_I
columns.description: A field observation by the police officer whether an intersection played a role in the crash. Does not represent whether or not the crash occurred within the intersection.

columns.name: CRASH_TYPE
columns.description: A general severity classification for the crash. Can be either Injury and/or Tow Due to Crash or No Injury / Drive Away

columns.name: REPORT_TYPE
columns.description: Administrative report type (at scene, at desk, amended)

columns.name: ROADWAY_SURFACE_COND
columns.description: Road surface condition, as determined by reporting officer

columns.name: CRASH_DATE_EST_I
columns.description: Crash date estimated by desk officer or reporting party (only used in cases where crash is reported at police station days after the crash)

columns.name: Boundaries - ZIP Codes
columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'Boundaries - ZIP Codes' (rpca-8um6) the point in column 'location' is located.  This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

columns.name: POSTED_SPEED_LIMIT
columns.description: Posted speed limit, as determined by reporting officer

columns.name: TRAFFIC_CONTROL_DEVICE
columns.description: Traffic control device present at crash location, as determined by reporting officer

columns.name: FIRST_CRASH_TYPE
columns.description: Type of first collision in crash

columns.name: TRAFFICWAY_TYPE
columns.description: Trafficway type, as determined by reporting officer

columns.name: LANE_CNT
columns.description: Total number of through lanes in either direction, excluding turn lanes, as determined by reporting officer (0 = intersection)

columns.name: ALIGNMENT
columns.description: Street alignment at crash location, as determined by reporting officer

columns.name: NUM_UNITS
columns.description: Number of units involved in the crash. A unit can be a motor vehicle, a pedestrian, a bicyclist, or another non-passenger roadway user. Each unit represents a mode of traffic with an independent trajectory.

columns.name: WEATHER_CONDITION
columns.description: Weather condition at time of crash, as determined by reporting officer

columns.name: INJURIES_UNKNOWN
columns.description: Total persons for whom injuries sustained, if any, are unknown

columns.name: DEVICE_CONDITION
columns.description: Condition of traffic control device, as determined by reporting officer

columns.name: DATE_POLICE_NOTIFIED
columns.description: Calendar date on which police were notified of the crash

columns.name: CRASH_MONTH
columns.description: The month component of CRASH_DATE.

columns.name: LOCATION
columns.description: The crash location, as determined by reporting officer, as derived from the reported address of crash, in a column type that allows for mapping and other geographic analysis in the data portal software

columns.name: CRASH_DAY_OF_WEEK
columns.description: The day of the week component of CRASH_DATE. Sunday=1

columns.name: LONGITUDE
columns.description: The longitude of the crash location, as determined by reporting officer, as derived from the reported address of crash

columns.name: WORKERS_PRESENT_I
columns.description: Whether construction workers were present in an active work zone at crash location

columns.name: LIGHTING_CONDITION
columns.description: Light condition at time of crash, as determined by reporting officer

columns.name: INJURIES_FATAL
columns.description: Total persons sustaining fatal injuries in the crash

columns.name: INJURIES_REPORTED_NOT_EVIDENT
columns.description: Total persons sustaining possible injuries in the crash as determined by the reporting officer. Includes momentary unconsciousness, claims of injuries not evident, limping, complaint of pain, nausea, and hysteria.

columns.name: LATITUDE
columns.description: The latitude of the crash location, as determined by reporting officer, as derived from the reported address of crash

columns.name: CRASH_HOUR
columns.description: The hour of the day component of CRASH_DATE.

columns.name: DAMAGE
columns.description: A field observation of estimated damage.

columns.name: HIT_AND_RUN_I
columns.description: Crash did/did not involve a driver who caused the crash and fled the scene without exchanging information and/or rendering aid

columns.name: NOT_RIGHT_OF_WAY_I
columns.description: Whether the crash begun or first contact was made outside of the public right-of-way.

columns.name: CRASH_DATE
columns.description: Date and time of crash as entered by the reporting officer

description: Crash data shows information about each traffic crash on city streets within the City of Chicago limits and under the jurisdiction of Chicago Police Department (CPD). Data are shown as is from the electronic crash reporting system (E-Crash) at CPD, excluding any personally identifiable information. Records are added to the data portal when a crash report is finalized or when amendments are made to an existing report in E-Crash. Data from E-Crash are available for some police districts in 2015, b

description: This information is derived from inspections of restaurants and other food establishments in Chicago from January 1, 2010 to the present. Inspections are performed by staff from the Chicago Department of Public Health’s Food Protection Program using a standardized procedure. The results of the inspection are inputted into a database, then reviewed and approved by a State of Illinois Licensed Environmental Health Practitioner (LEHP). name: Affordable Rental Housing Developments
description: The rental housing developments listed below are among the thousands of affordable units that are supported by City of Chicago programs to maintain affordability in local neighborhoods. The list is updated periodically when construction is completed for new projects or when the compliance period for older projects expire, typically after 30 years. The list is provided as a courtesy to the public. It does not include every City-assisted affordable housing unit that may be available for rent, nor

name: Lobbyist Data - Historical - Lobbyist Registry - 2010
description: All lobbyist registration fillings submitted to the Board of Ethics during the 2010 reporting period. / All lobbyists must register with the City of Chicago Board of Ethics by filing a Statement of Registration within five business days of engaging in lobbying activity and annually thereafter by January 20th. name: Chicago Street Names
description: List of all Chicago streets with suffixes and minimum and maximum address numbers.

columns.name: PERCENT AGED 25+ WITHOUT HIGH SCHOOL DIPLOMA
columns.description: Percent of persons over the age of 25 years without a high school education

columns.name: HARDSHIP INDEX
columns.description: Score that incorporates each of the six selected socioeconomic indicators (see dataset description)

columns.name: PERCENT AGED 16+ UNEMPLOYED
columns.description: Percent of persons over the age of 16 years that are unemployed

columns.name: PERCENT AGED UNDER 18 OR OVER 64
columns.description: Percent of the population under 18 or over 64 years of age (i.e., dependency)

columns.name: PERCENT HOUSEHOLDS BELOW POVERTY
columns.description: Percent of households living below the federal poverty level

columns.name: PER CAPITA INCOME
columns.description: Community Area Per capita income is estimated as the sum of tract-level aggregate incomes divided by the total population

columns.name: PERCENT OF HOUSING CROWDED
columns.description: Percent occupied housing units with more than one person per room

description: This dataset contains a selection of six socioeconomic indicators of public health significance and a “hardship index,” by Chicago community area, for the years 2008 – 2012. The indicators are the percent of occupied housing units with more than one person per room (i.e., crowded housing); the percent of households living below the federal poverty level; the percent of persons in the labor force over the age of 16 years that are unemployed; the percent of persons over the age of 25 years without

name: Business Licenses

columns.name: WARD PRECINCT
columns.description: The ward and precinct where the business is located. This column can be used to filter by precinct more easily across multiple wards.

columns.name: CONDITIONAL APPROVAL
columns.description: This pertains to applications that contain liquor licenses. Customers may request a conditional approval prior to building out the space.

columns.name: BUSINESS ACTIVITY ID
columns.description: A code for the business activity. Each ID value corresponds to a specific BUSINESS ACTIVITY value.

columns.name: COMMUNITY AREA
columns.description: Community Area Number

columns.name: APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS COMPLETE
columns.description: For all application types except RENEW, this is the date all required application documents were received.  For RENEW type records, this is the date the record was created.

columns.name: ACCOUNT NUMBER
columns.description: The account number of the business owner, which will stay consistent across that owner's licenses and can be used to find the owner in the Business Owners dataset.

columns.name: PRECINCT

name: Building Violations

columns.name: VIOLATION STATUS
INSPECTION CATEGORY
columns.description: Inspections are categorized by one of the following: COMPLAINT – Inspection is a result of a 311 Complaint. PERIODIC – Inspection is a result of recurring inspection (typically on an annual cycle). PERMIT – Inspection is a result of a permit. REGISTRATION – Inspection is a result of a Registration (typically Vacant Building Registration).

columns.name: PROPERTY GROUP
columns.description: Properties (lots) in the City of Chicago can typically have multiple point addresses, range addresses and buildings. Examples are corner lots, large lots, lots with front and rear buildings, etc. As a result, inspections (and their associated violations), permits and complaints related to a single property could have different addresses. This problem can be reconciled by using Property Group. All point and range addresses for a property are assigned the same Property Group key.

columns.name: Average Days to Complete Graffiti Removal
Median Days to Complete Graffiti Removal Request

description: The Department of Streets & Sanitation's (DSS) Graffiti Blasters crews offer a vandalism removal service to private property owners. This metric tracks the average number of days DSS takes to complete graffiti removal requests per week. Median days to complete requests as well as total number of requests fulfilled per week are available by mousing over columns. Currently the performance target for completing a graffiti removal request is 10 days. For more information on graffiti removal requests

description: Illinois Uniform Crime Reporting (IUCR) codes are four digit codes that law enforcement agencies use to classify criminal incidents when taking individual reports. These codes are also used to aggregate types of cases for statistical purposes. In Illinois, the Illinois State Police establish IUCR codes, but the agencies can add codes to suit their individual needs. columns.name: DOMESTIC
columns.description: Indicates whether the incident was domestic-related as defined by the Illinois Domestic Violence Act.

columns.name: LOCATION
columns.description: The location where the incident occurred in a format that allows for creation of maps and other geographic operations on this data portal. This location is shifted from the actual location for partial redaction but falls on the same block.

columns.name: CASE#
columns.description: The Chicago Police Department RD Number (Records Division Number), which is unique to the incident.

columns.name: DATE  OF OCCURRENCE
columns.description: Date when the incident occurred. This is sometimes a best estimate.

columns.name: ARREST
columns.description: Indicates whether an arrest was made.

columns.name: WARD
columns.description: The ward (City Council district) where the incident occurred. See the wards at https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/sp34-6z76.

columns.name: LONGITUDE
columns.description: The longitude of the location where the incident occurred. This location is shifted from the actual location for partial redaction but falls on the same block.

columns.name: LATITUDE
columns.description: The latitude of the location where the incident occurred. This location is shifted from the actual location for partial redaction but falls on the same block.

columns.name: SECONDARY DESCRIPTION
columns.description: The secondary description of the IUCR code, a subcategory of the primary description.

columns.name: X COORDINATE
columns.description: The x coordinate of the location where the incident occurred in State Plane Illinois East NAD 1983 projection. This location is shifted from the actual location for partial redaction but falls on the same block.

columns.name: BEAT
columns.description: Indicates the beat where the incident occurred. A beat is the smallest police geographic area - each beat has a dedicated police beat car. Three to five beats make up a police sector, and three sectors make up a police district. The Chicago Police Department has 22 police districts. See the beats at https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/aerh-rz74.

columns.name: Y COORDINATE
columns.description: The y coordinate of the location where the incident occurred in State Plane Illinois East NAD 1983 projection. This location is shifted from the actual location for partial redaction but falls on the same block.

columns.name: PRIMARY DESCRIPTION
columns.description: The primary description of the IUCR code.

columns.name: FBI CD
columns.description: Indicates the crime classification as outlined in the FBI's National Incident-Based Reporting System (NIBRS). See the Chicago Police Department listing of these classifications at https://gis.chicagopolice.org/pages/crime_details.

description: This dataset reflects reported incidents of crime (with the exception of murders where data exists for each victim) that have occurred in the City of Chicago over the past year, minus the most recent seven days of data. Data is extracted from the Chicago Police Department's CLEAR (Citizen Law Enforcement Analysis and Reporting) system. In order to protect the privacy of crime victims, addresses are shown at the block level only and specific locations are not identified. Should you have questions

name: Business Owners
description: This dataset contains the owner information for all the accounts listed in the Business License Dataset, and is sorted by Account Number. To identify the owner of a business, you will need the account number or legal name, which may be obtained from theBusiness Licenses dataset: https://data.cityofchicago.org/dataset/Business-Licenses/r5kz-chrr. Data Owner: Business Affairs & Consumer Protection. Time Period: 2002 to present. Frequency: Data is updated daily.

name: Public Passenger Vehicle Licenses

columns.name: Public Vehicle Number
columns.description: Assigned License Number. For Taxi type licenses, this number is also the corresponding medallion number.

columns.name: Record ID
columns.description: A unique ID for each license in this dataset.  It is a combination of the Public Vehicle Number and Vehicle Type.

columns.name: Status
columns.description: Status of the license.

columns.name: Company Name
columns.description: The legal owner/holder of the license.

columns.name: Address
columns.description: Company address

columns.name: City
columns.description: Company city

columns.name: ZIP Code
columns.description: Company ZIP Code

columns.name: Taxi Affiliation
columns.description: Only applies to Taxi type licenses. The association the taxi vehicle is affiliated with.

columns.name: Vehicle Fuel Source
columns.description: Type of fuel used by the vehicle associated with the license.

columns.name: Vehicle Color
columns.description: Color of the vehicle associated with the license.

columns.name: Vehicle Model Year
columns.description: Model year of the vehicle associated with the license.

columns.name: Vehicle Model
columns.description: Model of the vehicle associated with the license.

columns.name: Vehicle Make
columns.description: Make of the vehicle associated with the license.

columns.name: Taxi Medallion License Management
columns.description: Only applies to Taxi type licenses. Description of who manages the taxicab and medallion license.

columns.name: Wheelchair Accessible
columns.description: Only applies to Taxi type licenses. Yes or No to indicate if the taxi vehicle is a wheelchair accessible vehicle (WAV) or not.

columns.name: State
columns.description: Company state

columns.name: Vehicle Type
columns.description: Type of Public Passenger Vehicle license issued

description: A public passenger vehicle is a vehicle used for the transportation of passengers for hire by a public chauffeur. The list of public passenger vehicles includes licensed taxicabs (medallions), liveries, ambulances, medicars, charter-sightseeing buses, horse-drawn carriages, and pedicabs. For more information, please see https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/depts/bacp/provdrs/vehic.html.

description: Fire station locations

name: CTA - Ridership - Daily Boarding Totals
description: This dataset shows systemwide boardings for both bus and rail services provided by CTA, dating back to 2001. Daytypes are as follows: W = Weekday, A = Saturday, U = Sunday/Holiday. See attached readme file for information on how these numbers are calculated.

name: Traffic Crashes - Vehicles

columns.name: VEHICLE_YEAR
columns.description: The model year of the vehicle, if relevant

columns.name: UNIT_TYPE
columns.description: The type of unit

columns.name: UNIT_NO
columns.description: A unique ID for each unit within a specific crash report.

columns.name: CRASH_DATE
columns.description: Date and time of crash as entered by the reporting officer

columns.name: TOWED_TO
columns.description: Location to which the unit was towed, if relevant

columns.name: TOWED_BY
columns.description: Entity that towed the unit, if relevant

columns.name: EXCEED_SPEED_LIMIT_I
columns.description: Indicator of whether the unit was speeding, as determined by the reporting officer

columns.name: OCCUPANT_CNT
columns.description: The number of people in the unit, as determined by the reporting officer

columns.name: TOWED_I
columns.description: Indicator of whether the vehicle was towed

columns.name: MANEUVER
columns.description: The action the unit was taking prior to the crash, as determined by the reporting officer

columns.name: TRAVEL_DIRECTION
columns.description: The direction in which the unit was traveling prior to the crash, as determined by the reporting officer

columns.name: VEHICLE_USE
columns.description: The normal use of the vehicle, if relevant

columns.name: VEHICLE_TYPE
columns.description: The type of vehicle, if relevant

columns.name: LIC_PLATE_STATE
columns.description: The state issuing the license plate of the vehicle, if relevant

columns.name: MODEL
columns.description: The model of the vehicle, if relevant

columns.name: MAKE
columns.description: The make (brand) of the vehicle, if relevant

columns.name: NUM_PASSENGERS
columns.description: Number of passengers in the vehicle. The driver is not included. More information on passengers is in the People dataset.

columns.name: CRASH_UNIT_ID
columns.description: A unique identifier for each vehicle record.

columns.name: CRASH_RECORD_ID
columns.description: This number can be used to link to the same crash in the Crashes and People datasets. This number also serves as a unique ID in the Crashes dataset.

description: This dataset contains information about vehicles (or units as they are identified in crash reports) involved in a traffic crash. This dataset should be used in conjunction with the traffic Crash and People dataset available in the portal. “Vehicle” information includes motor vehicle and non-motor vehicle modes of transportation, such as bicycles and pedestrians. Each mode of transportation involved in a crash is a “unit” and get one entry here. Each vehicle, each pedestrian, each motorcyclist, a

columns.description: This column was automatically created in order to record in what polygon from the dataset 'Wards' (43wa-7qmu) the point in column 'location' is located. This enables the creation of region maps (choropleths) in the visualization canvas and data lens.

description: Chicago Public Library locations, contact information, and usual hours of operation. Please note that branches may experience short-term closures due to utility interruptions, HVAC problems, and other unforeseen issues. For more up-to-date hours, please see https://chipublib.bibliocommons.com/locations or the Hours & Locations link at the top of any page on https://www.chipublib.org. For current closures, please see https://www.chipublib.org/news/cpl-location-closures.

name: Sex Offenders
description: Description: Pursuant to the Sex Offender and Child Murderer Community Notification Law, 730 ILCS 152/101,et seq., the Chicago Police Department maintains a list of sex offenders residing in the City of Chicago who are required to register under the Sex Offender Registration Act, 730 ILCS 150/2, et seq. To protect the privacy of the individuals, addresses are shown at the block level only and specific locations are not identified. The data are extracted from the CLEAR (Citizen Law Enforcement An

name: Violence Reduction - Victims of Homicides and Non-Fatal Shootings

columns.name: INCIDENT_PRIMARY
columns.description: Text description of the IUCR Code that describes the major crime category it falls into. INCIDENT_PRIMARY represents the incident as a whole by conveying the most serious victimization according to IUCR hierarchy guidelines. In rare instances when we were not able to establish whether a non-fatal shooting victimization was an aggravated battery, robbery, or criminal sexual assault, we set this field equal to “NON-FATAL.”

columns.name: DISTRICT
columns.description: The current CPD district where the victimization occurred, using the boundaries in place as of 11/1/2021. Please refer to the City of Chicago’s Data Portal for a map of districts.

columns.name: STATE_HOUSE_DISTRICT
columns.description: Illinois House of Representatives Legislative District where the victimization occurred, using the boundaries in place as of 11/1/2021, represented by members elected to the Illinois House of Representatives.

columns.name: VICTIMIZATION_IUCR_SECONDARY
columns.description: IUCR Secondary adds more description to the Primary category.

columns.name: HOUR
columns.description: Hour of the day when the victimization occurred.

columns.name: AREA
columns.description: The CPD area where the victimization occurred, using the boundaries in place as of 11/1/2021. There are 5 CPD areas. Each area includes 3-4 police districts.

columns.name: BEAT
columns.description: The CPD beat where the victimization occurred, using the boundaries in place as of 11/1/2021. A beat is the smallest CPD geographic area. Each beat has a dedicated police beat car. Three to five beats make up a police sector, and three sectors make up a police district. The Chicago Police Department has 22 police districts. Please refer to the City of Chicago’s Data Portal for a map of beats.

columns.name: VICTIMIZATION_FBI_CD
columns.description: Crime classification as outlined in the FBI's Uniform Crime Reporting (UCR). See the Chicago Police Department listing of these classifications at http://gis.chicagopolice.org/clearmap_crime_sums/crime_type.

columns.name: HOMICIDE_VICTIM_FIRST_NAME
columns.description: (For homicide victims only) Victim's first name.

columns.name: UPDATED
columns.description: Records the most recent date the source table was updated.

columns.name: HOMICIDE_VICTIM_MI
columns.description: (For homicide victims only) Victim's middle initial.

columns.name: INCIDENT_FBI_CD
columns.description: Crime classification as outlined in the FBI's Uniform Crime Reporting (UCR), but only pertains to the most serious victimization in the incident. See the Chicago Police Department listing of these classifications at http://gis.chicagopolice.org/clearmap_crime_sums/crime_type.

columns.name: VICTIMIZATION_FBI_DESCR
columns.description: FBI Description connects a text description of the category to FBI Code.

columns.name: DAY_OF_WEEK
columns.description: Day of the week when the victimization occurred. Sunday=1

columns.name: DATE
columns.description: Date when the victimization occurred. This is sometimes a best estimate.

columns.name: INCIDENT_FBI_DESCR
columns.description: FBI Description connects a text description of the category to FBI Code, but only pertains to the most serious victimization in the incident.

columns.name: VICTIMIZATION_PRIMARY
columns.description: Text description of the IUCR Code that describes the major crime category it falls into. VICTIMIZATION_PRIMARY represents only the crime that this specific victim within the incident experienced. In rare instances when we were not able to establish whether a non-fatal shooting victimization was an aggravated battery, robbery, or criminal sexual assault, we set this field equal to “NON-FATAL.”

columns.name: SEX
columns.description: Sex of the victims. Victimization demographic data shown here are captured by CPD and limited to information included in the police report, which may not often be supported by individual self-identification including for sex/gender. In particular, CPD has historically recorded a victim’s sex rather than gender although has added a field for collecting gender as of January 2021.

columns.name: ZIP_CODE
columns.description: ZIP code where the victimization occurred, using the boundaries in place as of 11/1/2021.

columns.name: STREET_OUTREACH_ORGANIZATION
columns.description: Street outreach organization refers to organizations that actively work in “the streets” to engage individuals who are at immediate or high risk of being either victims or perpetrators of violence. Each organization has specific geographic boundaries where they focus their activities. This indicates which organization(s) is (are) currently active in the location where the victimization occurred, using the boundaries in place as of 11/1/2021, and may contain more than one organization as several outreach organizations’ boundaries overlap.

columns.name: AGE
columns.description: Age of the victims grouped by decade.

columns.name: STATE_SENATE_DISTRICT
columns.description: Illinois State Senate Legislative Districts (SLDs) where the victimization occurred, using the boundaries in place as of 11/1/2021, represented by members elected to the Illinois State Senate.

columns.name: RACE
columns.description: Crime classification as outlined in the FBI's Uniform Crime Reporting (UCR). See the Chicago Police Department listing of these classifications at http://gis.chicagopolice.org/clearmap_crime_sums/crime_type.

columns.name: INCIDENT_IUCR_SECONDARY
columns.description: Text description of the IUCR code that describes the sub category of the primary category. The column represents the incident as whole by conveying the most serious victimization according to IUCR hierarchy guidelines.

columns.name: MONTH
columns.description: Month when the victimization occurred.

columns.name: LOCATION
columns.description: A slightly altered location of the victimization in a form that allows for mapping and other geographic analysis on this data portal.

columns.name: INCIDENT_IUCR_CD
columns.description: The IUCR code that represents the most serious victimization experienced in this incident according to IUCR hierarchy guidelines. When we were not able to establish whether a homicide victimization was in the first or second degree, we set this field to “01XX.” When we were not able to establish whether a non-fatal shooting victimization was an aggravated battery, robbery, or criminal sexual assault, we set this field equal to “UNK.”

columns.name: CASE_NUMBER
columns.description: The Chicago Police Department RD Number (Records Division Number), which is a unique ID assigned to each incident. Due to an incident sometimes involving multiple victimizations, this number is repeated in this dataset for some incidents.

columns.name: BLOCK
columns.description: The partially redacted address where the victimization occurred, placing it on the same block as the actual address.

columns.name: UNIQUE_ID
columns.description: ID unique to each victimization.

columns.name: VICTIMIZATION_IUCR_CD
columns.description: Based on the Illinois Uniform Crime Reporting code. This is directly linked to the Primary Type and Description. See the list of IUCR codes at https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/c7ck-438e.

columns.name: HOMICIDE_VICTIM_LAST_NAME
columns.description: (For homicide victims only) Victim's last name.

columns.name: LATITUDE
columns.description: The latitude of the victimization location. In order to preserve anonymity, the given coordinates are not the actual location of the crime. To produce slightly altered coordinates, a circle roughly the size of an average city block was drawn around the original point location, and a new location was picked randomly from a spot around the circumference of that circle.

columns.name: WARD
columns.description: The City Council district in which the victimization occurred, using the boundaries in place as of 11/1/2021. Please refer to the city of Chicago’s Data Portal for a map of the 50 wards.

columns.name: GUNSHOT_INJURY_I
columns.description: Indicator field describing whether or not a victim was injured by gunfire. Non-fatal shooting data is not available before 2010 so all non-homicide victimizations will be recorded as “UNKNOWN.”

columns.name: COMMUNITY_AREA
columns.description: The community area where the victimization occurred. Chicago has 77 community areas. See the full list of Chicago’s community areas at https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/cauq-8yn6.

columns.name: LONGITUDE
columns.description: The longitude of the victimization location. This has been slightly altered to preserve anonymity. (see details under LATITUDE).

columns.name: LOCATION_DESCRIPTION
columns.description: Describes the location where a crime occurred, such as alley, sidewalk, etc.

description: This dataset contains individual-level homicide and non-fatal shooting victimizations, including homicide data from 1991 to the present, and non-fatal shooting data from 2010 to the present (2010 is the earliest available year for shooting data). This dataset includes a "GUNSHOT_INJURY_I " column to indicate whether the victimization involved a shooting, showing either Yes ("Y"), No ("N"), or Unknown ("UKNOWN.") For homicides, injury descriptions are available dating back to 1991, so the "shooti

description: As described in https://data.cityofchicago.org/stories/s/311-Dataset-Changes-12-11-2018/d7nq-5g7t, the function of this dataset was replaced by https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/v6vf-nfxy. This dataset is historical-only. All 311 calls for open and vacant buildings reported to the City of Chicago since January 1, 2010. The information is updated daily with the previous day's calls added to the records. The data set provides the date of the 311 service request and the unique Service Request # atta

name: Transportation Network Providers - Trips (2018 - 2022)

columns.name: Dropoff Centroid Longitude
columns.description: The longitude of the center of the dropoff census tract or the community area if the census tract has been hidden for privacy. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Pickup Centroid Longitude
columns.description: The longitude of the center of the pickup census tract or the community area if the census tract has been hidden for privacy. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Trips Pooled
columns.description: If customers were matched for a shared trip, how many trips, including this one, were pooled. All customer trips from the time the vehicle was empty until it was empty again contribute to this count, even if some customers were never present in the vehicle at the same time. Each trip making up the overall shared trip will have a separate record in this dataset, with the same value in this column.

columns.name: Pickup Centroid Latitude
columns.description: The latitude of the center of the pickup census tract or the community area if the census tract has been hidden for privacy. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Dropoff Centroid Latitude
columns.description: The latitude of the center of the dropoff census tract or the community area if the census tract has been hidden for privacy. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Shared Trip Authorized
columns.description: Whether the customer agreed to a shared trip with another customer, regardless of whether the customer was actually matched for a shared trip.

columns.name: Pickup Centroid Location
columns.description: The location of the center of the pickup census tract or the community area if the census tract has been hidden for privacy. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Dropoff Centroid Location
columns.description: The location of the center of the dropoff census tract or the community area if the census tract has been hidden for privacy. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Dropoff Census Tract
columns.description: The Census Tract where the trip ended. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Trip Miles
columns.description: Distance of the trip in miles.

columns.name: Pickup Census Tract
columns.description: The Census Tract where the trip began.This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Pickup Community Area
columns.description: The Community Area where the trip began. This column will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Dropoff Community Area
columns.description: The Community Area where the trip ended. This column will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Trip ID
columns.description: A unique identifier for the trip.

columns.name: Fare
columns.description: The fare for the trip, rounded to the nearest $2.50.

columns.name: Tip
columns.description: The tip for the trip, rounded to the nearest $1.00. Cash tips will not be recorded.

columns.name: Trip Start Timestamp
columns.description: When the trip started, rounded to the nearest 15 minutes.

columns.name: Trip End Timestamp
columns.description: When the trip ended, rounded to the nearest 15 minutes.

columns.name: Trip Seconds
columns.description: Time of the trip in seconds.

columns.name: Additional Charges
columns.description: The taxes, fees, and any other charges for the trip.

columns.name: Trip Total
columns.description: Total cost of the trip. This is calculated as the total of the previous columns, including rounding.

description: This dataset ends with 2022. Please see the Featured Content link below for the dataset that starts in 2023. All trips, from November 2018 to December 2022, reported by Transportation Network Providers (sometimes called rideshare companies) to the City of Chicago as part of routine reporting required by ordinance. Census Tracts are suppressed in some cases, and times are rounded to the nearest 15 minutes. Fares are rounded to the nearest $2.50 and tips are rounded to the nearest $1.00. For a dis

name: Chicago Traffic Tracker - Congestion Estimates by Segments

columns.name: LENGTH
columns.description: Length of the segment in miles.

columns.name: CURRENT_SPEED
columns.description: A value of -1 means no estimate is available.

columns.name: START_LONGITUDE
columns.description: The longitude associated with the starting point of the segment in the direction of traffic flow. For two-way streets it is roughly at the middle of the half that the segment is representing. For one-way streets this is the street center line. The startin

columns.name: STREET_HEADING
columns.description: The position of the segment in the address grid. North, South, East, or West of State and Madison.

columns.name: DIRECTION
columns.description: Traffic flow direction for the segment.

columns.name: SEGMENTID
columns.description: Unique arbitrary number to represent each segment.

columns.name: TO_STREET
columns.description: End street for the segment in the direction of traffic flow.

columns.name: FROM_STREET
columns.description: Start street for the segment in the direction of traffic flow.

columns.name: STREET
columns.description: Street name of the traffic segment.

description: This dataset contains the current estimated speed for about 1250 segments covering 300 miles of arterial roads. For a more detailed description, please go to https://tas.chicago.gov, click the About button at the bottom of the page, and then the MAP LAYERS tab. The Chicago Traffic Tracker estimates traffic congestion on Chicago’s arterial streets (nonfreeway streets) in real-time by continuously monitoring and analyzing GPS traces received from Chicago Transit Authority (CTA) buses. Two types of

name: COVID-19 Cases, Tests, and Deaths by ZIP Code - Historical

columns.name: Week Number
columns.description: A sequential count of weeks, starting at the beginning of 2020. These numbers are aligned to CDC MMWR weeks.

columns.name: ZIP Code
columns.description: Home ZIP Code of the cases and people tested.

columns.name: Deaths - Cumulative
columns.description: Number of deaths through the week.

columns.name: Cases - Weekly
columns.description: Number of cases in the week. Values are removed for privacy until the cumulative total for the ZIP Code has reached 5 cases. A blank indicates a suppressed number from 0 to 4.

columns.name: Tests - Weekly
columns.description: Number of tests in the week. Please note data limitations in the dataset description.

columns.name: Test Rate - Weekly
columns.description: Test rate per 100,000 population in the week. Because, as described in the dataset description, not all tests are reported, this rate is a low-end estimate. The true rate may be higher.

columns.name: Week Start
columns.description: The first date of the week.

columns.name: Percent Tested Positive - Weekly
columns.description: Percentage of tests returning positive results in the week based on specimen collection date. Because, as described in the dataset description, some not-positive tests may not be received, this rate is a high-end estimate. The true rate may be lower. Values are removed for privacy until the cumulative total for the ZIP Code has reached 5 cases.

columns.name: Case Rate - Cumulative
columns.description: Total case rate per 100,000 population through the week.

columns.name: Percent Tested Positive - Cumulative
columns.description: Percentage of tests returning positive results through the week based on specimen collection date. Because, as described in the dataset description, some not-positive tests may not be received, this rate is a high-end estimate. The true rate may be lower. Values are removed for privacy until the cumulative total for the ZIP Code has reached 5 cases.

columns.name: Week End
columns.description: The last date of the week.

columns.name: Deaths - Weekly
columns.description: Number of deaths in the week.

columns.name: ZIP Code Location
columns.description: A point within the ZIP Code to allow for geographic analysis. The precise point shown has no other meaning.

columns.name: Case Rate - Weekly

description: 311 Service Requests received by the City of Chicago. name: Payments

columns.description: DV indicates Direct Voucher. Direct Vouchers are used to pay for miscellaneous products and services that are not associated with a signed contract between the City and the Payee. Examples include debt service, utilities, third party payroll expenditures, court and legal settlements, and small payments such as travel reimbursements.

description: All vendor payments made by the City of Chicago from 1996 to present. Payments from 1996 through 2002 have been rolled-up and appear as "2002." “Current_Finding” means that even after COPA has completed an investigation and made a recommended finding, the finding can change given post-COPA processes, including Police Department review, Police Board adjudication, and grievance and arbitration processes.

description: This dataset is historical. For recent data, we recommend using https://chicagotraffictracker.com. -- Average Daily Traffic (ADT) counts are analogous to a census count of vehicles on city streets. These counts provide a close approximation to the actual number of vehicles passing through a given location on an average weekday. Since it is not possible to count every vehicle on every city street, sample counts are taken along larger streets to get an estimate of traffic on half-mile or one-mile

name: Parking Permit Zones

columns.name: ODD_EVEN
columns.description:
If 'E', only products whose address is both within this range AND address is even numbered will be offered zone.  
If 'O', only products whose address is both within this range AND address is odd numbered will be offered zone.

columns.name: BUFFER
columns.description:
Some segments are "Buffer": Parking is not restricted on the physical street, but residents who reside at an address within that range shall be eligible to purchase zoned products for nearby "Standard" segments where parking is restricted and signs are physically posted on the street. 
Y = The range is a buffer segment.  
N = The range is not a buffer segment, i.e., is a "standard" segment.

columns.name: ADDRESS RANGE - HIGH
columns.description: Higher value in the address range

columns.name: ZONE
columns.description: The zone number.  In cases where signs are posted (i.e., range is not a buffer range), this is the number that is listed on the physical sign.

columns.name: ADDRESS RANGE - LOW
columns.description: Lower value in the address range

columns.name: WARD - LOW
columns.description: The ward in which the low end of the address range falls.

columns.name: WARD - HIGH
columns.description: The ward in which the high end of the address range falls.

columns.name: STATUS
columns.description:
ACTIVE = currently affects product issuance (i.e., zoned products offered if product's address is within this zone record's range)
RESCINDED = does not affect product issuance.  The street range indicated by this record previously existed (i.e., signs used to be in the ground, or the range used to be a buffer), but between such time and present, the zone has been cancelled (with signs removed, etc.) pursant to City Council legislation or similar act.

columns.name: ROW ID
columns.description: Unique ID

name: Violence Reduction - Shotspotter Alerts - Historical

columns.name: INCIDENT_TYPE_DESCRIPTION
columns.description: ShotSpotter code describing the type of alert. Alert types are “Single Gunshot,” “Multiple Gunshot,” and “Gunshot or Firecracker.”

columns.name: HOUR
columns.description: The hour of the day when the alert was generated.

columns.name: ROUNDS
columns.description: Each ShotSpotter alert can contain multiple rounds if they are fired in quick succession. This column shows the number of rounds in the alert.

columns.name: MONTH
columns.description: The month when the alert was generated.

columns.name: LOCATION
columns.description: A slightly altered location of the alert in a form that allows for mapping and other geographic analysis on this data portal.

columns.name: BEAT
columns.description: The CPD beat where the potential gunfire was detected. A beat is the smallest CPD geographic area. Each beat has a dedicated police beat car. Three to five beats make up a police sector, and three sectors make up a police district. The Chicago Police Department has 22 police districts. Please refer to the City of Chicago’s Data Portal for a map of beats.

columns.name: AREA
columns.description: The CPD area where the potential gunfire was detected. There are 5 CPD areas. Each area includes 3-4 police districts.

columns.name: LONGITUDE
columns.description: The longitude of the potential gunfire detection. This has been slightly altered to preserve anonymity (see details under LATITUDE).

columns.name: ILLINOIS_SENATE_DISTRICT
columns.description: The Illinois State Senate district in which the alert occurred.

columns.name: ZIP_CODE
columns.description: The zip code where the potential gunfire was detected.

columns.name: COMMUNITY_AREA
columns.description: The community area where the potential gunfire was detected. Chicago has 77 community areas. See the map of Chicago’s community areas at https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/cauq-8yn6.

columns.name: LATITUDE
columns.description: The latitude of the potential gunfire detection. In order to preserve anonymity, the given coordinates are not the actual location of the crime. To produce slightly altered coordinates, a circle roughly the size of an average city block was drawn around the original point location, and a new location was picked randomly from a spot around the circumference of that circle.

columns.name: ILLINOIS_HOUSE_DISTRICT
columns.description: The Illinois State House of Representatives district in which the alert occurred.

columns.name: UNIQUE_ID
columns.description: The unique identifier generated by shotspotter for each alert.

columns.name: STREET_OUTREACH_ORGANIZATION
columns.description: Street outreach organization refers to organizations that actively work in “the streets” to engage individuals who are at immediate or high risk of being either victims or perpetrators of violence. Each organization has specific geographic boundaries where they focus their activities. This indicates which organization(s) is (are) active in the location where the ShotSpotter alert occurred and may contain more than one organization as several outreach organizations’ boundaries overlap.

columns.name: DISTRICT
columns.description: The CPD district where the potential gunfire was detected. Please refer to the City of Chicago’s Data Portal for a map of districts.

columns.name: WARD
columns.description: The City Council district in which the potential gunfire was detected. Please refer to the city of Chicago’s Data Portal for a map of the 50 wards.

columns.name: BLOCK
columns.description: The block of the address where ShotSpotter detected potential gunfire.

columns.description: Date when the alert was generated.

description: The following dataset includes "Active Benchmarks," which are provided to facilitate the identification of City-managed standard benchmarks. Standard benchmarks are for public and private use in establishing a point in space. Note: The benchmarks are referenced to the Chicago City Datum = 0.00, (CCD = 579.88 feet above mean tide New York). The City of Chicago Department of Water Management’s (DWM) Topographic Benchmark is the source of the benchmark information contained in this online database.

columns.description: The street direction for the permit location. For multiple locations on a permit, there will be multiple rows in the dataset.

columns.description: Type of street closure, if any, associated with the location.

columns.description: Detail information about the permit application.

columns.description: If any fees were waived for this permit, the amount waived will be here. Possible reasons for waived fees include city contracts, city departments, ordinances, and franchise agreements with the City.

columns.description: Person to contact in an emergency as listed by the applicant during the permit application process.

columns.name: LASTINSPECTIONNUMBER
columns.description: Once a permit is issued, typically an inspection is created for CDOT Inspectors to use onsite. Once an inspection is completed, another inspection may be generated or if the work is finished, the permit is closed. This is the number of the last inspection.

columns.name: APPLICATIONSTATUS
columns.description: Overall status of the application. Typically open during the application process and issued, then closed once the permit has expired.

columns.name: WORKTYPEDESCRIPTION
columns.description: Sub-category, if any, of the permit application. Only one work type per application is allowed.

columns.name: APPLICATIONDESCRIPTION
columns.description: Type of permit.

columns.name: APPLICATIONNAME
columns.description: Applicants can put a project name on their applications in this field. For converted data 1/1/2013 to 11/13/2015, the old permit number will be placed in this field.

columns.name: LATITUDE
columns.description: Latitude of the start of the address range.

columns.name: APPLICATIONSTARTDATE
columns.description: Permit start date. Most often requested by the applicant but could be updated as the permit is reviewed by CDOT.

columns.name: PRIMARYCONTACTSTREET2
columns.description: Street address 2nd line of the primary applicant.

columns.name: PRIMARYCONTACTCITY
columns.description: City of the primary applicant.

columns.name: PRIMARYCONTACTSTATE
columns.description: State of the primary applicant.

columns.name: UNIQUEKEY
columns.description: A unique identifier for each record.

columns.name: APPLICATIONNUMBER
columns.description: Unique number for the application that becomes the permit number when issued.

columns.name: APPLICATIONTYPE
columns.description: Code for the type of permit. Permits may be categorized further by the WORKTYPE.

columns.name: WORKTYPE
columns.description: Code for the sub-category, if any, of the permit application. Only one work type per application is allowed.

columns.name: APPLICATIONENDDATE
columns.description: Permit End date. Most often requested by the applicant but could be updated as the permit is reviewed by CDOT.

columns.name: APPLICATIONPROCESSEDDATE
columns.description: This is the date when the permit reaches the review milestone, means the date CDOT began reviewing the application.

columns.name: PRIMARYCONTACTZIP
columns.description: ZIP Code of the primary applicant.

columns.name: APPLICATIONISSUEDDATE
columns.description: Date the permit was issued by CDOT. Set once all the reviews, fees, etc. are paid (where applicable).

columns.name: APPLICATIONFINALIZEDDATE
columns.description: Date the permit was closed (either after the permit has expired plus a grace period or after an inspector marks the work complete).

columns.name: APPLICATIONEXPIREDATE
columns.description: Date the permit will expire (typically same as permit end date). Applicant may ask for date extensions where the end date is updated and will be reflected here once re-approved by CDOT.

columns.name: CURRENTMILESTONE
columns.description: The current step in the permitting life cycle.

columns.name: TOTALFEES
columns.description: Total permit fees for this permit calculated based on the work being carried out and location. See fee schedule for further information, available on the CDOT permit portal, http://www.cityofchicago.org/city/en/depts/cdot/provdrs/construction_information/svcs/online-permit-portal.html.

columns.name: COMMENTS

columns.name: PRIMARYCONTACTLAST

columns.name: PRIMARYCONTACTFIRST
columns.description: First name of the primary applicant, if not a company.

columns.name: PRIMARYCONTACTMIDDLE
columns.description: Middle initial of the primary applicant, if not a company.

columns.name: PRIMARYCONTACTSTREET
columns.description: Street address of the primary applicant.

columns.description: Code for the type of inspection generated for the issued permit. Typically this will match the type of permit although special inspection types are available for stop work.

columns.description: Description of the last inspection completed against this permit.

columns.name: LASTINSPECTIONDATE

columns.name: LASTINSPECTIONRESULT

name: Chicago Energy Benchmarking

columns.name: # of Buildings
columns.description: Number of buildings included in the property's report. In cases where a property is reporting as a campus, multiple buildings may be included in one report.

columns.name: Exempt From Chicago Energy Rating
columns.description: Shows whether the building is subject to the Chicago Energy Rating Ordinance. Some properties are required to submit energy benchmarking reports but are not subject to the requirements of the Chicago Energy Rating program. These buildings do not receive a Chicago Energy Rating, typically due to technical reasons.  This column was added for the 2018 Data Year. It is blank for previous years.

columns.name: Primary Property Type
columns.description: The primary use of a property (e.g. office, retail store). Primary use is defined as a function that accounts for more than 50% of a property. This is the Property Type - EPA Calculated field from Portfolio Manager.

columns.name: Gross Floor Area - Buildings (sq ft)
columns.description: Total interior floor space in square feet between the outside surfaces of a building’s enclosing walls, with the exception of any interior space used for parking. This includes tenant space, common areas, stairwells, basements, and storage, but does not include any interior parking areas. This is the Property Gross Floor Area - EPA Calculated (Buildings) field from Portfolio Manager.

columns.name: Source EUI (kBtu/sq ft)
columns.description: Source Energy Use Intensity (EUI) is a property's Source Energy Use divided by its gross floor area. Source Energy Use is the annual energy used to operate the property, including losses from generation, transmission, & distribution. Source EUI is  measured in thousands of British thermal units (kBtu) per square foot.

columns.name: GHG Intensity (kg CO2e/sq ft)
columns.description: Total Greenhouse Gas Emissions divided by property's gross floor area, measured in kilograms of carbon dioxide equivalent per square foot.

columns.name: Location
columns.description: Property location in a data type recognized by the data portal software for mapping and other purposes.

columns.name: Natural Gas Use (kBtu)
columns.description: The annual amount of utility-supplied natural gas consumed by the property, measured in thousands of British thermal units (kBtu).

columns.name: Weather Normalized Source EUI (kBtu/sq ft)
columns.description: Weather Normalized (WN) Source Energy Use Intensity (EUI) is a property's WN Source Energy divided by its gross floor area. WN Source Energy is the Source Energy Use the property would have consumed during 30-year average weather conditions. WN Source EUI is measured in measured in thousands of British thermal units (kBtu) per square foot.

columns.name: Latitude
columns.description: Property latitude.

columns.name: Total GHG Emissions (Metric Tons CO2e)
columns.description: The total amount of greenhouse gas emissions, including carbon dioxide, methane, and nitrous oxide gases released into the atmosphere as a result of energy consumption at the property, measured in metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalent.

columns.name: Property Name
columns.description: Official or common property name.

columns.name: Year Built
columns.description: Year in which a property was constructed or underwent a complete renovation.

columns.name: Data Year
columns.description: Calendar year (January-December) represented by each data record.

columns.name: Chicago Energy Rating
columns.description: The zero-to-four-star Chicago Energy Rating assigned to the building in the shown Data Year. A building with zero stars did not submit a report, or did submit a report but was missing required information. All other buildings receive between one and four stars, with four stars reflecting the highest performance. Every building receives a Chicago Energy Rating Placard with this rating, which must be posted in a prominent location at the building. The rating must also be shared at the time of listing the building for sale or for lease. For more information, visit: www.ChicagoEnergyRating.org  This column was added for the 2018 Data Year. It is blank for previous years.

columns.name: All Other Fuel Use (kBtu)
columns.description: The annual amount of fuel use other than electricity, natural gas, district chilled water, or district steam consumed by the property on-site, measured in thousands of British thermal units (kBtu).

columns.name: Row_ID
columns.description: A unique ID for the row, made up of the combination of the Data Year and ID.

columns.name: Longitude
columns.description: Property longitude.

columns.name: Weather Normalized Site EUI (kBtu/sq ft)
columns.description: Weather Normalized (WN) Site Energy Use Intensity (EUI) is a property's WN Site Energy divided by its gross floor area (in square feet). WN Site Energy is the Site Energy Use the property would have consumed during 30-year average weather conditions. WN Site EUI is measured in measured in thousands of British thermal units (kBtu) per square foot.

columns.name: Site EUI (kBtu/sq ft)
columns.description: Site Energy Use Intensity (EUI) is a property's Site Energy Use divided by its gross floor area. Site Energy Use is the annual amount of all the energy consumed by the property on-site, as reported on utility bills. Site EUI is  measured in thousands of British thermal units (kBtu) per square foot.

columns.name: District Chilled Water Use (kBtu)
columns.description: The annual amount of district chilled water consumed by the property on-site, measured in thousands of British thermal units (kBtu).

columns.name: District Steam Use (kBtu)
columns.description: The annual amount of district steam consumed by the property on-site, measured in thousands of British thermal units (kBtu).

columns.name: Electricity Use (kBtu)
columns.description: The annual amount of electricity consumed by the property on-site, including electricity purchased from the grid and generated by onsite renewable systems, measured in thousands of British thermal units (kBtu).

columns.name: Water Use (kGal)
columns.description: Water use for the data year in thousands of gallons. This column was added for the 2018 Data Year. It is blank for previous years. This information is collected by the City of Chicago from the Chicago Department of Water and is not reported by building owners to the City of Chicago. In some cases, data are not available for some buildings.

columns.name: Community Area
columns.description: The Chicago community area where the property is located.

columns.name: ENERGY STAR Score
columns.description: 1-100 rating that assesses a property’s overall energy performance, based on national data to control for differences among climate, building uses, and operations. A score of 50 represents the national median.

columns.name: Address
columns.description: Property street address.

columns.name: Reporting Status
columns.description: Whether the building submitted its report for the Data Year shown. Buildings that did not do so were not listed at all for Data Years through 2017 so all records prior to 2018 will have a value of Submitted. "Temporarily Exempt" means that the building received a one-year, temporary exemption from reporting. "Not Submitted"  means that the building did not receive an exemption or submit a report, and is out of compliance. This status could also mean that a report was submitted, but was missing required information, such as whole-building energy use. "Submitted" means the building submitted a report and is in compliance for the Data Year shown.

columns.name: ID
columns.description: A six-digit unique identifier assigned to each property covered by the Chicago Energy Benchmarking Ordinance for tracking and identification purposes.

columns.name: ZIP Code
columns.description: Property ZIP code.

columns.description:
0   = No precipitation 
60 = Liquid precipitation, e.g. rain

70 = Solid precipitation, e.g. snow 
40 = unspecified precipitation

description: The Chicago Park District maintains weather sensors at beaches along Chicago's Lake Michigan lakefront. These sensors generally capture the indicated measurements hourly while the sensors are in operation during the summer. During other seasons and at some other times, information from the sensors may not be available. See https://data.cityofchicago.org/id/qmqz-2xku for a dataset with similar measurements on the lake water, itself. The sensor locations are listed at https://data.cityofchicago.or

description: This dataset lists monthly station entry averages, by day type (Weekday, Saturday or Sunday/Holiday), as well as monthly totals, beginning in 2001. Note that some stations (such as on the Cermak Branch--now Pink Line) and Skokie did not have Saturday and/or Sunday/holiday service until more recent years, although, in cases where weekday service ran past midnight, late evening fares may appear as part of Saturday tallies.

name: Transportation Network Providers - Drivers

columns.name: EMPLOYMENT_CLASSIFICATION
columns.description: Employee or Independent Contractor. In the event of different TNPs reporting different values for the same driver, Employee takes priority over Independent Contractor. This data element was not collected before October 2020.

columns.name: CITY
columns.description: The driver's city of residence.

columns.name: DRIVER_START_MONTH
columns.description: The month in which the driver was reported to have started. If different companies reported different dates, the earliest one is used.

columns.name: ZIP
columns.description: The driver's ZIP Code of residence.

columns.name: MULTIPLE_TNPS
columns.description: Whether the driver was reported by multiple TNPs in this month. Matching is imperfect so some driver records that should have been combined may be separate.

columns.name: NUMBER_OF_TRIPS
columns.description: Number of trips provided in this month. Due to the complexities of matching, errors are possible in both directions. Values over 999 are converted to null as suspected error values that interfere with easy data visualization.

columns.name: BIRTH_YEAR
columns.description: The year the driver was born. Years that would result in ages below 21 or above 125 have been nulled out as presumed errors.

columns.name: AGE
columns.description: Calculated as the difference between the year portion of MONTH_REPORTED and BIRTH_YEAR. Therefore, it will be one year higher than the driver's true age in some cases. Ages below 21 and above 125 have been nulled out as presumed errors.

columns.name: STATE
columns.description: The state of the driver's driver's license.

columns.name: MONTH_REPORTED
columns.description: The month in which the driver was reported. If a driver is reported in multiple months, which is common, he or she will have multiple records -- one for each month.

All drivers reported by Transportation Network Providers (sometimes called rideshare companies) to the City of Chicago as part of the licensing process and routine reporting required by ordinance. name: Transportation Network Providers - Trips (2023-2024)

columns.name: Trip End Timestamp
columns.description: When the trip ended, rounded to the nearest 15 minutes.

columns.name: Dropoff Centroid Longitude
columns.description: The longitude of the center of the dropoff census tract or the community area if the census tract has been hidden for privacy. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Dropoff Centroid Latitude
columns.description: The latitude of the center of the dropoff census tract or the community area if the census tract has been hidden for privacy. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Pickup Centroid Longitude
columns.description: The longitude of the center of the pickup census tract or the community area if the census tract has been hidden for privacy. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Trip Seconds
columns.description: Time of the trip in seconds.

columns.name: Trip Start Timestamp
columns.description: When the trip started, rounded to the nearest 15 minutes.

columns.name: Dropoff Centroid Location
columns.description: The location of the center of the dropoff census tract or the community area if the census tract has been hidden for privacy. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Pickup Census Tract
columns.description: The Census Tract where the trip began.This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Pickup Centroid Location
columns.description: The location of the center of the pickup census tract or the community area if the census tract has been hidden for privacy. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Shared Trip Authorized
columns.description: Whether the customer agreed to a shared trip with another customer, regardless of whether the customer was actually matched for a shared trip.

columns.name: Dropoff Community Area
columns.description: The Community Area where the trip ended. This column will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Trip Total
columns.description: Total cost of the trip. This is calculated as the total of the previous columns, including rounding.

columns.name: Fare
columns.description: The fare for the trip, rounded to the nearest $2.50.

columns.name: Dropoff Census Tract
columns.description: The Census Tract where the trip ended. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Percent Distance Chicago
columns.description: NEW IN THIS DATASET: Percent of the trip distance that was in Chicago.

columns.name: Additional Charges
columns.description: The taxes, fees, and any other charges for the trip.

columns.name: Trip ID
columns.description: A unique identifier for the trip.

columns.name: Percent Time Chicago
columns.description: NEW IN THIS DATASET: Percent of the trip time that was in Chicago.

columns.name: Pickup Community Area
columns.description: The Community Area where the trip began. This column will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Trips Pooled
columns.description: If customers were matched for a shared trip, how many trips, including this one, were pooled. All customer trips from the time the vehicle was empty until it was empty again contribute to this count, even if some customers were never present in the vehicle at the same time. Each trip making up the overall shared trip will have a separate record in this dataset, with the same value in this column.

columns.name: Tip
columns.description: The tip for the trip, rounded to the nearest $1.00. Cash tips will not be recorded.

columns.name: Pickup Centroid Latitude
columns.description: The latitude of the center of the pickup census tract or the community area if the census tract has been hidden for privacy. This column often will be blank for locations outside Chicago.

columns.name: Trip Miles
columns.description: Distance of the trip in miles.

columns.name: Shared Trip Match
columns.description: NEW IN THIS DATASET: Whether the customer was actually matched to a shared trip.

Real-time estimated speed in miles per hour. For congestion advisory and traffic

description: This dataset contains the historical estimated congestion for 1270 traffic segments, in selected time periods from August 2011 to May 2018. Newer records are in https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/sxs8-h27x. The most recent estimate for each segment is in https://data.cityofchicago.org/d/n4j6-wkkf. The Chicago Traffic Tracker estimates traffic congestion on Chicago’s arterial streets (non-freeway streets) in real-time by continuously monitoring and analyzing GPS traces received from Chicago Transit

name: Violence Reduction - Victim Demographics - Aggregated

columns.name: SEX
columns.description: The sex of the victims. In particular, CPD has historically recorded a victim’s sex rather than gender although has added an additional field for collecting gender as of January 2021.

columns.name: NUMBER_OF_VICTIMS

columns.name: GUNSHOT_INJURY_I

columns.name: DOMESTIC_I

columns.name: TIME_PERIOD_START

columns.name: TIME_PERIOD

columns.name: AGE
columns.description: The age of the victims grouped by decade.

columns.name: PRIMARY_TYPE

columns.name: JUVENILE_I
columns.description: Whether the victims were juveniles (under age 18). A blank value means Unknown.

description: This dataset contains aggregate data on violent index victimizations at the quarter level of each year (i.e., January – March, April – June, July – September, October – December), from 2001 to the present (1991 to present for Homicides), with a focus on those related to gun violence. Index crimes are 10 crime types selected by the FBI (codes 1-4) for special focus due to their seriousness and frequency.
